news.title: Senior NIH scientist, research fellow charged with bringing deactivated mpox virus into U.S.
news.summary: A senior NIH scientist and his research fellow were charged with smuggling vials of deactivated mpox virus into the country from Africa and lying about it.

news.title: STAT+: Longevity startup NewLimit raises $435 million ahead of first clinical trial
news.summary: Longevity startup NewLimit plans to launch its first clinical trial of a liver medicine after raising a staggering $435 million in new funding.

news.title: STAT+: Radiopharmaceutical shows promise in post-Pluvicto setting
news.summary: Abivax's ulcerative colitis data, Fulcrum's scrapped sickle cell drug, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Lilly threat to 340B hospitals, Ebola vaccine funding, and more
news.summary: Lilly gave hospitals participating in a federal drug discount program five days to submit claims data or it will stop the price breaks

news.title: How one state’s medical meal program lowered health care costs
news.summary: Medicaid work requirements, fast-tracked funding for experimental Ebola vaccines, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Online care is caught in the crossfire as states crack down on corporate medicine
news.summary: Growing legislative scrutiny across multiple states targets the corporate structure underlying most direct-to-consumer telehealth businesses.

news.title: Opinion: The virtual end of the doctor’s office waiting room
news.summary: It’s time to rethink and redesign how patients enter the health care system altogether, writes emergency physician Iyesatta Massaquoi Emeli.

news.title: STAT+: Trump administration releases rules for new Medicaid work requirements
news.summary: States are racing to implement the new requirements by the start of next year.

news.title: STAT+: Eli Lilly warns hospitals to submit claims data in the next five days or lose their 340B drug discounts
news.summary: Eli Lilly has told more than 1,000 hospitals participating in the 340B drug discount program to submit claims data over the next five days — or lose price breaks.

news.title: STAT+: Global coalition to fast-track three vaccines targeting Ebola outbreak with $62 million in funding
news.summary: With no licensed vaccines available to protect against the Ebola virus currently spreading in the Congo, efforts are underway to fast-track development of at least three vaccines.

news.title: STAT+: Abivax ulcerative colitis drug shows strong efficacy, but cases of cancer raise concerns
news.summary: Abivax said its experimental treatment for ulcerative colitis showed significant efficacy in a closely watched trial, but shares of the company tanked on safety concerns.

news.title: STAT+: At ASCO, talk of barriers to cancer care, new treatments, and other big takeaways
news.summary: Here is some of what you may have missed from the world's largest cancer research meeting.

news.title: STAT+: At the world’s largest cancer research meeting, data briefly took a backseat to grief
news.summary: At the world's largest cancer research meeting, new data were plentiful. But this year, there was also a focus on grief.

news.title: As FDA misses deadline on electric shock ban, disability advocates speak out
news.summary: The FDA has missed a self-imposed deadline to ban the use of electrical shock devices in people with intellectual disabilities.

news.title: STAT+: Medicare’s mum on GLP-1s, and Massachusetts sues United
news.summary: We've been asking Medicare for weeks about the cost of giving seniors $50 obesity drugs.

news.title: Microsoft, Mayo Clinic plan to build frontier AI model for healthcare
news.summary: Mayo Clinic plans to develop and deploy a frontier AI model specifically designed for healthcare in collaboration with Microsoft.

news.title: Industry groups say final Medicaid work requirements rule imposes onerous documentation burden
news.summary: On Monday, the Trump administration finalized a key rule that establishes national guidelines for the rollout of work requirements in Medicaid. Industry groups warn that the regulation could prove burdensome to enrollees and state programs.

news.title: OIG: Feds may have overpaid MA plans by millions due to unsupported stroke diagnoses
news.summary: A new federal report suggests that Medicare Advantage plans may have earned millions in overpayments tied to unsupported stroke diagnoses.

news.title: FTC clears Ascension's $3.9B AmSurg purchase, but requires some ASC divestitures
news.summary: The federal regulator said it will permit a deal to create the country's third-largest ASC platform to go forward, so long as seven specific ASCs in threatened markets are divested.

news.title: Wearable adoption climbs 33% in past decade but users still tend to be younger, healthier: Rock Health
news.summary: Forty-six percent of respondents in the 2025 Consumer Adoption of Digital Health Survey reported owning a wearable specifically, and 57% of respondents reported owning at least one wearable or other connected device.

news.title: Joint Commission launches voluntary AI certification program for healthcare organizations
news.summary: Any healthcare organization may apply for the certification, and interested organizations do not have to be accredited by the Joint Commission.

news.title: Eli Lilly's ultimatum to hospitals: Send 340B claims data by June 8 or lose discounts
news.summary: The drugmaker has issued a June 8 ultimatum to an unspecified number of hospitals that have resisted a data submission policy it implemented in February. Furious hospital industry groups are pushing back, calling the decision unlawful and urging the administration to intervene.

news.title: Wolters Kluwer Health survey examines AI use and concerns among clinicians, patients in 2026
news.summary: For physicians, repeated daily AI use tripled between 2025 and 2026 while use among nurses doubled during the same period.

news.title: Northwell's firearm injury risk screener now widely available within Epic
news.summary: The screening tool, now in the electronic health record, combines assessment of firearm access and community violence risk.

news.title: AI-powered medical imaging startup Subtle Medical picks up $33M and taps new CEO
news.summary: Subtle Medical's AI software improves image quality and accelerates scans on existing imaging systems, helping providers increase throughput without purchasing new hardware.

news.title: CMS outlines national framework to support rollout of Medicaid work requirements
news.summary: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that delineates key details for states in the nationwide rollout of work requirements in Medicaid.

news.title: CDC: 2025's uninsured rate stayed flat at 8.3%
news.summary: An annual federal survey found about 28 million uninsured individuals in 2025, up from 27.2 million in the previous year.

news.title: New heights for NewLimit as anti-aging biotech nabs $435M to rejuvenate old cells
news.summary: Anti-aging startup NewLimit is back with a fresh fundraise of $435 million after landing on its first clinic-ready candidate years earlier than expected.

news.title: ASCO: Roche, head held high, details oral SERD's first-line flop in breast cancer
news.summary: To Roche, a phase 3 flop in first-line breast cancer does not erase the megablockbuster potential of its oral SERD drug giredestrant. Instead, the Swiss pharma has been inspired to open a new phase 3 study in the adjuvant setting.

news.title: Foundation Medicine tallies FDA nod to pair its blood test with Pfizer’s prostate cancer combo
news.summary: Foundation Medicine tallied another nod from the FDA for one of its genomic profiling tests that searches for specific biomarkers in tumor tissue samples.

news.title: Travere scales Everest with $1.1B deal for BTK inhibitor aimed at rare kidney diseases
news.summary: Travere Therapeutics has set its sights on Everest Medicines' therapy for rare kidney diseases, handing over $112.5 million upfront for a drug that has already begun the trek into the clinic.

news.title: ASCO: Cardiff posts updated colorectal cancer data ahead of pivotal push
news.summary: Cardiff Oncology has shared updated results from its phase 2 colorectal cancer trial, revealing that the study drug has yet to reach the median progression-free survival as the biotech prepares to enter pivotal development.

news.title: Fulcrum scraps SCD program, considers options after FDA takes hard line on safety risks
news.summary: Fulcrum Therapeutics has scrapped its sole clinical-stage program after the FDA took a hard line on the modality following the recent withdrawal of Ipsen’s Tazverik from the U.S. market.

news.title: MindMaze Therapeutics culls program, ends legacy ops from Relief merger to cut costs
news.summary: Digital therapeutics specialist MindMaze Therapeutics is selling off a pipeline program while also removing all non-neurology legacy operations from its recent merger with Relief Therapeutics.

news.title: ASCO: Celcuity dares to dream of becoming $10B company after breast cancer win over Piqray
news.summary: While Revolution Medicines dominated this year’s American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting by conquering the “undruggable” RAS pathway, another up-and-coming biotech hopes to do the same with a different vexing target that drives a plethora of cancers.

news.title: ASCO: Bristol Myers’ $800M bispecific ADC aces China breast cancer study, putting TROP2 drugs on notice
news.summary: The first-in-class EGFRxHER3 bispecific ADC that BMS paid $800 million for has shown strong phase 3 results from China that suggest it could put up a fight against blockbuster TROP2 agents in triple-negative breast cancer.

news.title: Abivax stock suffers as cancer cases overshadow stellar efficacy in ulcerative colitis phase 3 trial
news.summary: Abivax has raised the efficacy bar in ulcerative colitis, linking obefazimod to a 40%, placebo-adjusted jump in clinical remission rates in a phase 3 trial. But the share price of the biotech, a reported takeover target for several Big Pharmas, collapsed after several patients on the high dose developed cancers.

news.title: Praxis pauses one of its seizure programs after phase 2 fail
news.summary: Praxis Precision Medicines’ epilepsy pipeline has suffered a setback after a next-gen NaV blocker failed to sufficiently reduce seizures in a phase 2/3 study.

news.title: ASCO: Takeda touts ‘tremendous potential’ of Innovent’s PD-1/IL-2 bispecific after seeing survival data
news.summary: Takeda and Innovent's long-term follow-up data from an early study of their collaborative PD-1/IL-2 bispecific colors the "tremendous potential" of the asset, Takeda's P.K. Morrow said in an interview.

news.title: AI 教父下场创业：硅谷开始押注“下一代 AI 范式”
news.summary: LeCun创AMI，另辟蹊径研发世界模型探索AGI

news.title: AI体检：有人赚得盆满钵满，有人只剩营销噱头
news.summary: 体量庞大且保持增长的体检市场，正迎来AI技术的全面渗透。

news.title: 集体爆发，4年来首次
news.summary: 资金加速回流

news.title: 大厂开医院哪家强？
news.summary: 刘强东比张一鸣快一步。

news.title: Coding拷问一切，独立大模型赛道的两年生死（上篇）
news.summary: 中国独立大模型从AI六小龙到四家Coding争位市值分化

news.title: 瑞士医疗科技企业PeriVision研发AI视功能检查算法及检测仪，提升青光眼检测速度 | 瑞士创新100强
news.summary: 比传统SITA标准程序快70%。

news.title: 连续三年市占率第一，国产手术机器人冲刺IPO
news.summary: 真健康凭先发技术卡位，赴港IPO待商业化验证

papers.title: AutoMedBench: Towards Medical AutoResearch with Agentic AI Models
papers.summary: Autonomous agents are increasingly expected to support end-to-end medical-AI research workflows, moving beyond isolated prediction tasks or short-form clinical question answering. However, existing medical agent benchmarks primarily evaluate final outputs, providing limited visibility into agent behavior within the research process. To address this gap, we present AutoMedBench, a workflow-aware benchmark for autonomous medical-AI research across diverse medical imaging and multimodal inference tasks, organizing agent execution into a unified five-stage workflow (S1-S5): Plan, Setup, Validate,

papers.title: Diagnosing Harmful Continuation in Answer-Correct Long-CoT Training Traces
papers.summary: Long chain-of-thought (CoT) traces are widely used as supervision for reasoning-oriented LLM SFT, yet answer-correct traces can still lead to markedly different fine-tuning outcomes. We study post-conclusion continuation in answer-correct long-CoT data: a continuation where the answer appears sufficiently supported, but the trace continues with additional reasoning that remains in the supervised target. To test its training effect, we use a delete-only editor to construct answer-preserving suffix removal and compare CoT-based SFT on the original and processed traces. We observe improved SFT ou

papers.title: Pressure-Testing Deception Probes in LLMs: Scaling, Robustness, and the Geometry of Deceptive Representations
papers.summary: Linear probes trained on LLM activations are increasingly proposed as deception-detection metrics, yet report AUROC exceeding 0.96 on clean benchmarks while collapsing under distributional shift. This paper systematically pressure-tests probe-based metrics across the Gemma 3 model family (1B-27B parameters), diagnosing why they fail rather than merely documenting that they fail. We test four hypotheses about deception encoding: (1) single linear direction, (2) multi-dimensional subspace, (3) convex conic hull, (4) entropy proxy. Our design includes cross-domain transfer matrices, multi-dimensi

papers.title: Benchmarking Visual State Tracking in Multimodal Video Understanding
papers.summary: Understanding a video requires more than recognizing isolated moments, as humans continuously track entities, states, and events over time. This capacity for visual state tracking is fundamental to video understanding, yet remains underexplored in current evaluations of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). We introduce Visual STAte Tracking benchmark (VSTAT), a video-based benchmark designed to diagnose visual state tracking in MLLMs. VSTAT consists of 834 clips drawn from both synthetic and real-world videos, paired with 1,500 questions that cannot be answered from any single frame or sh

papers.title: Same Question, Different Source, Different Answer: Auditing Source-Dependence in Medical Multi-Source RAG
papers.summary: A retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system deployed over a multi-author institutional corpus can give a different answer to the same question depending on which source it retrieves -- a failure mode the dominant single-gold-answer paradigm cannot diagnose. We argue that source-dependence is a missing axis of NLP evaluation, and that auditing it means shifting the unit of evaluation from answer correctness to the inter-source relationship. We make this concrete in transplant patient education, where institutional sources demonstrably disagree, releasing three artefacts: TransplantQA, a bench
